source_pr.title: *: fix data race in the IgnoreTruncate
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #42341

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

fix the data race and do some optimization for bazel's test
### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: DATA RACE on `StmtCtx.IgnoreTruncate`: a statement-context flag was a plain bool accessed concurrently by multiple goroutines/tests.
failure_signature: DATA RACE (race detector): concurrent access to `StmtCtx.IgnoreTruncate`
root_cause_explanation: The flake was caused by a data race on `StmtCtx.IgnoreTruncate`: the flag was a plain bool but was read/written across different code paths (and tests) that can run concurrently. This PR fixes the race by changing `IgnoreTruncate` to an `atomic.Bool` and updating all reads/writes to use `Load()`/`Store()` (with proper save/restore via `defer` in call sites).
fix_pattern: For statement/session context flags that may be accessed concurrently, use atomic/mutex-protected state (`atomic.Bool` + `Load`/`Store`) instead of plain booleans; always restore the original value in `defer` when tests temporarily override it.

evidence: evidence: *: fix data race in the IgnoreTruncate
